subject: Operating Systems
description: Processes & threads, CPU scheduling, memory management, deadlocks, and concurrency.

questions.question: Which CPU scheduling algorithm can cause starvation of low-priority processes?
questions.explanation: Under priority scheduling, low-priority processes may wait indefinitely if higher-priority processes keep arriving. Aging is used to prevent this.

questions.question: Which of these is one of the four Coffman conditions necessary for a deadlock?
questions.explanation: The four conditions are mutual exclusion, hold and wait, no preemption, and circular wait.

questions.question: Thrashing in an operating system refers to:
questions.explanation: Thrashing occurs when the system spends more time swapping pages in and out of memory than executing processes.

tests.title: Operating Systems — Mock Test 1
tests.description: 30-question timed test covering scheduling, memory management, deadlocks, and concurrency.

studyMaterials.title: OS Concepts — Quick Reference
studyMaterials.description: Concise notes on CPU scheduling algorithms, memory management, deadlock conditions, and page replacement.

subject: DBMS
description: Normalization, indexing, transactions, ACID properties, and SQL query reasoning.

questions.question: Which normal form eliminates transitive dependencies?
questions.explanation: Third Normal Form removes transitive dependencies of non-prime attributes on the primary key.

questions.question: In ACID, what does the 'D' stand for?
questions.explanation: Durability guarantees that once a transaction commits, its changes survive system failures.

questions.question: Which statement about a primary key is true?
options: It must be a foreign key
questions.explanation: A primary key must be unique and non-NULL, uniquely identifying each row in a table.

tests.title: DBMS — Mock Test 1
tests.description: Covers normalization forms, ACID properties, SQL joins, indexing, and transaction management.

studyMaterials.title: DBMS — Quick Reference Notes
studyMaterials.description: Compact notes on normal forms, ER diagrams, SQL queries, and transaction isolation levels.

subject: Computer Networks
description: OSI & TCP/IP layers, routing, common protocols, and IP addressing.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for routing packets?
questions.explanation: The Network layer (Layer 3) handles logical addressing and routing of packets between hosts.

questions.question: Which of the following is a connection-oriented protocol?
questions.explanation: TCP establishes a connection via a three-way handshake and guarantees ordered, reliable delivery.

questions.question: How many bits make up an IPv4 address?
questions.explanation: IPv4 addresses are 32 bits, usually written as four 8-bit octets (e.g. 192.168.0.1).

tests.title: Computer Networks — Mock Test 1
tests.description: Covers OSI & TCP/IP layers, routing protocols, IP addressing, subnetting, and common application-layer protocols.

studyMaterials.title: Computer Networks — Quick Reference
studyMaterials.description: Layer-by-layer OSI notes, TCP vs UDP comparison, IP addressing cheat sheet, and protocol summaries.

subject: OOP Concepts
description: Encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction, and SOLID basics.

questions.question: Which OOP principle hides internal state and exposes access only through methods?
questions.explanation: Encapsulation bundles data with the methods that operate on it and restricts direct access to internal state.

questions.question: Method overriding is an example of which type of polymorphism?
questions.explanation: Overriding is resolved at run time based on the actual object type (dynamic dispatch).

questions.question: In the SOLID principles, the 'S' stands for:
questions.explanation: The Single Responsibility Principle states that a class should have only one reason to change.

tests.title: OOP Concepts — Mock Test 1
tests.description: Covers the four pillars of OOP, SOLID principles, design patterns basics, and class/object relationships.

studyMaterials.title: OOP & SOLID — Quick Reference
studyMaterials.description: Concise explanations of encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction, and SOLID principles with examples.

subject: Data Structures
description: Arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs, hashing, and complexity analysis.

questions.question: What is the average-case time complexity of search in a balanced binary search tree?
questions.explanation: A balanced BST has height O(log n), so search, insert, and delete are O(log n) on average.

questions.question: Which data structure follows First-In-First-Out (FIFO) ordering?
questions.explanation: A queue removes elements in the same order they were added — FIFO. A stack is LIFO.

questions.question: What is the average-case lookup time of a hash table?
questions.explanation: With a good hash function and low load factor, hash table lookups are O(1) on average.

tests.title: Data Structures — Mock Test 1
tests.description: Covers arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, hashing, and Big-O complexity analysis.

studyMaterials.title: Data Structures — Quick Reference
studyMaterials.description: Cheat sheet covering time/space complexities of common data structures and key operations.

subject: System Design Basics
description: Scaling, caching, load balancing, and high-level design trade-offs.

questions.question: Which technique reduces database read load by storing frequent results in fast memory?
questions.explanation: Caching keeps frequently accessed data in fast storage (e.g. Redis) to avoid repeated expensive reads.

questions.question: A load balancer primarily helps with:
questions.explanation: A load balancer spreads incoming requests across multiple servers to improve availability and throughput.

questions.question: Horizontal scaling refers to:
options: Adding more CPU/RAM to one server
questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling (scaling out) adds more machines, whereas vertical scaling adds resources to one machine.

tests.title: System Design — Mock Test 1
tests.description: Covers caching, load balancing, horizontal vs vertical scaling, CAP theorem, and microservices basics.

studyMaterials.title: System Design Basics — Quick Reference
studyMaterials.description: Overview of core system design concepts: caching, sharding, replication, load balancing, and CAP theorem.
